task_completion.description: Open the NOAA OI SST dataset in a visualization tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A data visualization application (like Panoply) is open, showing the dataset 'sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' loaded in its file/dataset browser.

task_completion.description: Generate a spatial map of the tropical Pacific SST.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D spatial map plot window is visible showing Sea Surface Temperature (SST), with the map boundaries focused on the tropical Pacific Ocean region.

task_completion.description: Export the spatial map to the ENSODiagnostic directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager shows the file 'tropical_pacific_sst_july.png' being saved to or existing in the '/home/ga/Documents/ENSODiagnostic/' directory.

task_completion.description: Generate an equatorial line plot of SST vs. longitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 1D line plot window is visible showing Sea Surface Temperature on the y-axis and Longitude on the x-axis, representing an equatorial profile.

task_completion.description: Export the line plot to the ENSODiagnostic directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager shows the file 'equatorial_sst_profile_july.png' being saved to or existing in the '/home/ga/Documents/ENSODiagnostic/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a formal diagnostic report identifying the ENSO phase.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the contents of 'enso_baseline_report.txt', which explicitly mentions the ENSO phase as 'NEUTRAL', or a file manager shows this file in the '/home/ga/Documents/ENSODiagnostic/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI application (like Panoply) to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with a graphical data visualization tool's interface to create the plots, rather than using terminal commands or running pre-written scripts to bypass GUI interaction.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to write the diagnostic report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a GUI text editor (e.g., gedit, mousepad, nano in terminal) being used to compose the report, rather than just using 'echo' commands to blindly pipe text into a file.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided local dataset rather than searching the web.
integrity.visual_evidence: The plots and data must clearly originate from the local '/home/ga/PanoplyData/sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' file. There should be no evidence of the agent downloading pre-made images from a web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct dataset is '/home/ga/PanoplyData/sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc'. The expected outputs must be saved in '/home/ga/Documents/ENSODiagnostic/' and include exactly: 'tropical_pacific_sst_july.png', 'equatorial_sst_profile_july.png', and 'enso_baseline_report.txt'. The correct ENSO phase to be identified in the report is 'NEUTRAL' because the data is a long-term mean (1991-2020) climatological baseline.
